title: 6 Dishonoured Cheques that are Re-entered through the Clearing System Procedures
body_text:
6	Dishonoured Cheques that are Re-entered through the Clearing System Procedures
6.1	Overview of Cheques that are Re-entered through the Clearing System
The practice of returning a cheque through the clearing system a second time is generally prohibited by CPA Rule A4, Section 8(a), although there are some exceptions.
Returned items can be re-entered into the clearing system by the Negotiating Financial Institution if
the Drawee branch provides written authorization to re-clear the item;
the Drawee branch returns the item without enough information to identify the Payee;
a Pre-authorized Debit (PAD) is re-presented for payment according to CPA Rule F1, Re-presentment and Rejected Returned Transactions;
a cheque used to pay for a bill payment is dishonoured;
the Drawee branch returned the item for the reason ГўВҖВңitem cleared in wrong currencyГўВҖВқ or ГўВҖВңitem incorrectly amount-encodedГўВҖВҷ and the Negotiating Financial Institution is exchanging the item in the correct currency and amount; or
the Drawee branch returned the item for the reason ГўВҖВңimage missing or not usableГўВҖВқ or ГўВҖВңCRD or image MICR mismatchГўВҖВқ, and the Negotiating Financial Institution is providing a new CRD or image in place of the old one.
If the customerГўВҖВҷs financial institution accepts a cheque from the customer in payment of a bill payment for a Biller with a CCIN and the cheque is subsequently dishonoured by the drawee, the drawee institution can return the dishonoured cheque to the BillerГўВҖВҷs financial institution for reimbursement, but only if:
The amount of the dishonoured cheque corresponds with the amount paid.
The BillerГўВҖВҷs CCIN and the customerГўВҖВҷs account number with the Biller are correctly and legibly recorded on the back of the dishonoured cheque.
The dishonoured cheque is returned to the BillerГўВҖВҷs financial institution no later than the business day following receipt.
The dishonoured cheque is returned using a Returned Item envelope pursuant to CPA Rule A4, Returned and Redirected Items. The reason for return must be identified as ГўВҖВңPursuant to Rule H6ГўВҖВқ.
Refer to CPA Rule H6, S. 23, Clearing and Settlement of Dishonoured Items, for more information.
If the Negotiating Financial Institution believes that an item wasnГўВҖВҷt returned in accordance with the CPA rules, that item is an Item in Dispute, and canГўВҖВҷt be re-entered into the clearing system a second time.
6.2	Refusing Chargebacks
If the financial institution (as the Negotiating Financial Institution) receives a Chargeback and doesnГўВҖВҷt agree with the return for any reason other than those specified in CPA Rule A4, Section 8(a), that item is an Item in Dispute.
CPA Rule A6, Items in Dispute, outlines the procedures by which a financial institution can place or resolve an Item in Dispute. Items in Dispute must be negotiated outside of the clearing system.
Proceed as follows to dispute a Chargeback:
Prepare a letter to the Drawee Financial Institution stating the reason for refusal of the returned cheque. See Sample Letter ГўВҖВ“ Reason for Refusal, Form 6082.
Forward the letter and a copy of the front and back of the cheque image to the Drawee Financial Institution by registered mail.
Retain copies for your records.
The Item in Dispute is to remain suspended pending resolution of the dispute.
Related Information
Sample Letter ГўВҖВ“ Reason for Refusal,
Form 6082
6.3	Refusing a Cheque that was Re-entered into the Clearing System
If your financial institution (as the Drawee branch) returns an item, and it is re-entered into the clearing system by the Negotiating Financial Institution for any reason other than those mentioned in CPA Rule A4, Section 8(a), proceed as follows:
Debit the Negotiating Financial Institution. Include details of the item stating that the Negotiating Financial Institution has contravened CPA Rule A4.
Return a copy of the item along with a cover letter to the Negotiating Financial Institution by registered or certified mail.
Note:	If a Negotiating Financial Institution sends correspondence to your financial institution stating that it believes your financial institution has lost recourse on a specific cheque, you must prove that recourse hasnГўВҖВҷt been lost. If you canГўВҖВҷt prove that recourse hasnГўВҖВҷt been lost, immediate settlement must be provided to the Negotiating Financial Institution.
END
